EF1 TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FROM APPROXIMATELY 110 TO 115 PM. THE INITIAL DAMAGE STARTED JUST SOUTH AND WEST OF THE INTERSECTIONS OF HIGHWAYS 96 AND 49. THIS ON THE WESTERN EDGE OF THE TOWN OF VIRGILINA. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY ONE MILE TRAVELING TO THE NORTHEAST. AT THE WIDEST POINT...THE TORNADO WAS 240 YARDS WIDE.6 HOMES WERE DAMAGED...AND NUMEROUS LARGE TREES WERE DOWNED AND SNAPPED.
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN ON HILL MOUNTAIN ROAD.

EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NORTH OF FIGSBORO NEAR STATE ROUTE 886 IN HENRY COUNTY AND MOVED NORTH FOR APPROXIMATELY 1 MILE INTO FRANKLIN COUNTY BEFORE LIFTING NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF STATE ROUTE 618 AND STATE ROUTE 632. THE MAXIMUM WIDTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 75 YARDS. NUMEROUS TREES WERE DOWNED WITH 4 HOMES BEING DAMAGED ALONG THE TORNADO PATH.

AN EF0 TORNADO...WITH PEAK WINDS BETWEEN 70 AND 80 MPH...FIRST TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF WELCH ROAD AND BLUEFIELD BLVD. A NARROW AND VERY SPORADIC SWATH OF TORNADO DAMAGE WAS FOUND SOUTHEAST ENDING ON ROBIN HOOD ROAD ABOUT A MILE AND A HALF AWAY AROUND 730 PM. THE WIDTH OF THE DAMAGE WAS LESS THAN 60 YARDS AT ITS GREATEST. THE DAMAGE WAS CONFINED TO TREES BLOWN DOWN...SOME ON HOMES AND CARS.
